question: The house-filtered v7 retraining produced amorphous blobs with severe overfitting (train/val gap = 0.14). Is this diagnostic evidence that the continuous latent diffusion pipeline is fundamentally broken, or was the test compromised by confounds?

project_state.status_snapshot: Phase 4.9. House-filtered v7 retrained on structures tagged 'house' from the CSV dataset (6,174 entries after tag filtering). Results: amorphous blobs again, with severe overfitting (train/val gap = 0.14). This is the 8th attempt at continuous latent diffusion. The project has a systematic pattern of running confounded experiments and then debating the confounds.

prior_decisions.title: Tag filtering
prior_decisions.outcome: CSV House tags filter (6,174 entries) as cheaper pre-check before manual curation

metrics.model_size: 3.5M parameters — potentially oversized for filtered dataset
metrics.tag_quality: CSV 'house' tags are noisy — many tagged structures are not visually houses
metrics.attempts: 8 continuous latent diffusion versions, 0 coherent outputs

project_state.version_history: v1-v7: all failed (mode collapse, adversarial collapse x3, empty, incoherent, blobs x2) → scope-reduction: invalid test → house-filtered: blobs + overfitting

confounds_discovered_later: Noisy tag labels — many structures tagged 'house' in the CSV are not visually houses, so the model was trained on heterogeneous data
Model too large — 3.5M parameters for a filtered dataset causes overfitting rather than learning structure
Wrong checkpoint selection — may not have used best-validation-loss checkpoint for generation
good_references: Pattern of confounded experiments — scope reduction had broken criteria, house-filtered has noisy labels + oversized model + overfitting
The project has spent 4 debates designing scope reduction tests and 4 times run them with multiple confounds — this is a process failure
failed_approaches_to_avoid: Using tag-based filtering without visual verification of label quality
Training an oversized model (3.5M params) on a small filtered dataset
Running experiments with multiple uncontrolled variables simultaneously
ground_truth.actual_decision: One final clean test: manually curate 30-50 ACTUAL houses by visual inspection, retrain with reduced model ([32,64,128] channels, ~800K params), generate from best-val checkpoint, visual inspection only (no automated criteria). 4-hour time cap. If blobs: pivot to discrete diffusion. If blurry houses: post-hoc guidance. If ambiguous: treat as failure and pivot.
ground_truth.actual_outcome: The final clean test was designed to be the first confound-free experiment the project has ever run.
